name: Happy Cat Public Storefront Host

securitySheaf.label: Probe IC
securitySheaf.effect: Host starts checking the intruder icon and access path.

securitySheaf.label: Trace IC
securitySheaf.effect: Host attempts to establish or accelerate a trace.

securitySheaf.label: Scramble IC
securitySheaf.effect: Host interferes with files, memory, or the current operation.

securitySheaf.label: Store-brand Tar Baby
securitySheaf.effect: Nuisance IC attempts to hold the icon in place for follow-up.

sculpting: Bright retail storefront, mascot popups, collectible-card catalog, snack ads, register hooks, employee stockroom doors, and local teen chatter overlays.
notes: First live lead from Session 2026-07-09. Small-business host near dense public Matrix traffic. Tuned as the starter example for 1-4 featured Decker Experience actions plus GM-adjudicated custom actions.

nodes.title: Local Grid Outside Happy Cat
nodes.description: You hover in the east Nashville grid among cheap public icons, snack ads, apartment-block chatter, and the glowing storefront sign for Happy Cat.

choices.label: Open the Happy Cat public door
Study the traffic before entering
Skim the public catalog from the street
Look for staff-access seams from outside

nodes.title: Mascot Storefront
nodes.description: A lucky-cat mascot bows from behind a neon counter. Card packs orbit like menu icons; snack ads crawl along the floor like animated receipt tape.

choices.label: Browse cards, snacks, and event postings
Search customer chatter and teen hangout traces
Look for staff-only doors
Back out to the local grid

nodes.title: Catalog Aisles and Event Shelf
nodes.description: Collectible inventory, snack specials, event flyers, trading-card binders, and public stock notices sit under too many cute sale banners.

choices.label: Cross-reference event postings with teen traffic
Check inventory gaps for Pixel Sticks-shaped noise
Trace vendor and restock breadcrumbs
Return to the mascot counter

nodes.title: Sticker-Covered Traffic Cloud
nodes.description: Speech bubbles, sticker tags, purchase pings, and public-grid noise float around the storefront. Most of it is teenage pose and retail spam, but patterns may be hiding in the clutter.

choices.label: Filter for Pixel Sticks-shaped traces
Identify frequent local handles near the storefront
Enter the public storefront
Slip away before the public grid gets curious

nodes.title: Customer Chatter Boards
nodes.description: Message-board cards flip past: collectible trades, snack complaints, local gossip, and repeated absences where vandal tags should have been.

choices.label: Pursue the missing-tag gap
Identify repeat teen handles and hangout times
Try to reach staff records
Return to the catalog shelves

nodes.title: Single Staff Pinpad Door
nodes.description: Behind the cute storefront is a single staff-only access surface: one employee pinpad, one locked stockroom icon, and no obvious side menu.

choices.label: Open the staff-only lock

nodes.title: Locked Stockroom Door
nodes.description: Camera logs, shift notes, vendor orders, and back-office records are stacked behind a sealed stockroom interface shaped like card boxes.

choices.label: Open staff records carefully
Spoof a store system command
Read vendor-order labels through the crack
Return to public storefront

nodes.title: Back Office Cabinet
nodes.description: Receipts, employee notes, camera thumbnails, and supplier records sit in neat rows. The useful question is not what was hit, but why this place was not.

choices.label: Copy suspicious camera/receipt slices
Check device hooks
Audit vendor orders and stock anomalies
Plant or alter a harmless-looking store note

nodes.title: Vendor Orders and Restock Queue
nodes.description: Supplier pings, restock queues, snack invoices, and collectible shipments form a mundane little paper trail with surprisingly useful timing data.

choices.label: Compare delivery timing against missing vandal tags
Copy supplier and restock anomalies
Return to the back-office cabinet

nodes.title: Register and Camera Hooks
nodes.description: The register, door chime, camera feed, and display shelves hang as simple device icons. They are mundane, but mundane things remember who walked past.

choices.label: Pull camera feed pointers
Spoof the door chime or display shelf briefly
Evade rising attention

nodes.title: Neon Static Where Tags Should Be
nodes.description: The absence has a shape: a clean patch in the vandalism wave, too neat to be pure luck. Either someone avoided Happy Cat, protected it, or used it as a marker.

choices.label: Compare against back-office records
Follow the static toward possible Pixel Sticks traces
Cross-check the gap against vendor timing
Fight through store-brand nuisance IC if it notices

nodes.title: Useful Pattern Cache
nodes.description: You have enough strands to hand the GM a concrete lead: traffic, receipts, cameras, vendor timing, or missing-tag geometry pointing to the next branch of the run.

choices.label: Log off cleanly
Keep digging and risk the sheaf
Use the lead to open the staff door if it is still available

nodes.title: Clean Exit
nodes.description: The storefront folds back into neon signs and public chatter. If the tally stayed low, nobody knows the mascot let you look behind the counter.
